title: Bytes, Unicode, Newlines, Front Matter, Metadata, and Schemas · Contract Workshop
summary: A civic-publishing pair incrementally builds versioned decoded-document and metadata contract from original fixed content fixtures while retaining earlier Python, Git, HTML, CSS, URL, accessibility, security, determinism, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned decoded-document and metadata contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Decode source bytes with declared UTF-8 and error policy while preserving path, byte count, newline behavior, and causal diagnostics.
Choose normalization, identifier, comparison, sorting, and display policies while retaining original author text.
Recognize bounded delimiters, separate metadata bytes from body, reject malformed closure, and avoid executing content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Explicit Decoding
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop publishing team handles case 1280 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode UTF-8 content and validate bounded front matter without hiding data loss; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Decode source bytes with declared UTF-8 and error policy while preserving path, byte count, newline behavior, and causal diagnostics.

For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Explicit Decoding, keep Explicit Decoding connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-time-language-identity-1xicw6
# Competency: ssg-time-language-identity.
# Case 4381: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_time_language_identity_1xicw6(byte_count: int = 240, decoded_units: int = 228, metadata_fields: int = 6, decode_errors: int = 0, schema_errors: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1xicw6 = "1xicw6-6wcix1-4381"
    assert evidence_variant_1xicw6
    evidence_axes_1xicw6 = (
        "reader1xicw6",
        "source4381",
        "parse6wcix1",
        "route1xicw64381",
        "artifact43811xicw6",
        "recovery6wcix14381",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1xicw6)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_units < 0:
        return False, decoded_units, "text-count"
    if decoded_units > byte_count:
        return False, decoded_units, "encoding-units"
    if metadata_fields < 3:
        return False, metadata_fields, "metadata-shape"
    if decode_errors > 0 or schema_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors + schema_errors, "invalid-document"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_units
    return True, metadata_fields, "validated-document"
